skill.name: longbridge-derivatives
skill.description:
Options chains, option quotes, option volume, Greeks (Delta/Gamma/Theta/Vega), implied volatility, and HK warrants (callable bull/bear, call/put warrants, issuer list) for HK/US markets via Longbridge.
Triggers: "期权", "期权链", "认购", "认沽", "行权价", "到期日", "IV", "隐含波动率", "Greeks", "delta", "gamma", "窝轮", "牛熊证", "认购证", "认沽证", "認購", "認沽", "行權價", "隱含波動率", "窩輪", "牛熊證", "option", "option chain", "call", "put", "strike", "expiry", "implied volatility", "warrant", "CBBC", "期權", "期權鏈"
skill.summary: Read-only options and HK warrants data via the Longbridge CLI — chains, quotes, Greeks, IV, strategies, and P&L frameworks for HK and US markets.
skill.author: longbridge

security_audit.summary: Prompt-only skill with no executable scripts. All 86 'backtick execution' flags are markdown inline-code spans (e.g. `longbridge option --help`) in documentation, not code execution. 'Weak cryptographic algorithm' at SKILL.md:3 is a false match on the YAML frontmatter (words like 'metadata', 'model'). 'System reconnaissance' flags match the math term 'std' (standard deviation) in volatility formulas. 'Configuration library' is a false match in a workflow description. No scripts, no network code, no file writes, no env access. Verified by reading SKILL.md and reference files.

evidence.description: Markdown inline-code spans containing longbridge CLI examples like `longbridge option --help`. These are documentation references, not executed code.

low_findings.title: Static analyzer false positives: backtick patterns
low_findings.description: 86 'Ruby/shell backtick execution' flags are all markdown inline-code spans in documentation. The skill instructs the LLM to call the longbridge CLI; the backticks are formatting, not code execution. No code is embedded in the skill itself.

low_findings.title: Static analyzer false positive: weak crypto at SKILL.md:3
low_findings.description: 'Weak cryptographic algorithm' match on the YAML frontmatter (line 3 contains the description field, not cryptographic code). The match is on substrings like 'metadata' or 'model'.

low_findings.title: Static analyzer false positive: system reconnaissance
low_findings.description: Matches on the math abbreviation 'std' (standard deviation) in HV/IV formulas and the word 'system' in normal documentation. Not actual system reconnaissance.

content.user_title: Query options chains and HK warrants through Longbridge
content.value_statement: Traders and analysts need fast access to options chains, Greeks, implied volatility, and HK warrant data without leaving their AI assistant. This skill routes natural-language questions to the Longbridge CLI and returns structured results.
actual_capabilities: Look up option quotes with full Greeks (Delta, Gamma, Theta, Vega) for HK and US underlyings
Browse option chains by expiry date and strike price
Retrieve real-time call/put volume snapshots for sentiment analysis
Compute and compare implied volatility vs historical volatility (60-day HV)
Show IV percentile rank, volatility smile, and put/call skew
List HK warrants and callable bull/bear contracts (CBBC) with issuer information
limitations: Read-only — does not place or modify orders (mutating trading requires a separate account skill)
Public data only for the option and warrant subcommands; no account positions
US options require US market access entitlement on the Longbridge account
HV/IV percentile calculations are approximate proxies when a full historical IV series is unavailable

use_cases.title: Options chain lookup for a US stock
use_cases.description: A retail trader asks for the AAPL option chain expiring this Friday, including Greeks and IV. The skill calls longbridge option chain and longbridge option quote to return a structured chain.

use_cases.title: HK warrant screening
use_cases.description: A Hong Kong investor wants to compare callable bull/bear contracts (CBBC) and warrants on 700.HK from different issuers. The skill returns the warrant list and issuer breakdown.

use_cases.title: IV vs HV volatility briefing
use_cases.description: A quant analyst requests an implied-volatility vs historical-volatility snapshot for TSLA.US with smile and skew, to decide between premium-selling and premium-buying strategies.

prompt_templates.title: Get an option chain
prompt_templates.prompt: Show me the option chain for TSLA.US expiring on 2025-01-17, with strike, last price, IV, delta, and volume for both calls and puts.

prompt_templates.title: Quote a specific OCC option symbol
prompt_templates.prompt: Quote AAPL240119C190000 with all Greeks and open interest.
prompt_templates.scenario: Trader has a specific OCC contract in mind

prompt_templates.title: HK warrant screening
prompt_templates.prompt: List the top 5 bull CBBCs on 700.HK by issuer, with effective gearing and call price.

prompt_templates.title: Volatility analysis
prompt_templates.prompt: Compute IV vs 60-day HV for NVDA.US, show the smile across strikes, and tell me whether the put skew is elevated.
prompt_templates.scenario: Vol analyst building a strategy view

output_examples.input: Compare IV vs HV for TSLA.US
output: TSLA.US volatility snapshot — Source: Longbridge Securities
IV/HV ratio: 1.18 → IV is rich relative to recent realized vol
Put skew: +6 pp → market is pricing meaningful downside
Suggested read: premium-selling structures may have an edge

best_practices: Run `longbridge <subcommand> --help` first to confirm the exact flags for the current CLI version before issuing a call
Fetch the option chain without a date first to discover available expiries, then re-query with --date for the specific expiry you want
Compare ATM IV against a 60-day HV proxy from the daily kline to judge whether vol is rich or cheap before recommending a strategy
Cite Longbridge Securities as the source on every output and keep the response language matching the user's input
anti_patterns: Do not hard-code OCC option symbols when the user only knows the underlying — use the two-step chain → quote discovery flow
Do not invent strike prices, Greeks, or IV values — always pull from `longbridge option` or `longbridge option quote`
Do not recommend placing orders from this skill — it is read-only; route order intents to the trading skill

faq.question: Is this skill read-only or can it place trades?
faq.answer: Read-only. It only fetches option quotes, chains, volume, and warrant data. Order placement belongs to a separate mutating account skill.

faq.question: Which markets are supported?
faq.answer: Hong Kong and US options, plus Hong Kong warrants and CBBC. US options require US market access on the Longbridge account.

faq.question: Do I need to log in to use this skill?
faq.answer: No login is required for the option and warrant subcommands. They return public market data.

faq.question: How is implied volatility calculated?
faq.answer: Implied volatility is sourced from Longbridge's option chain for each strike. Historical volatility is a 60-day annualised standard deviation of daily log returns from the kline endpoint.

faq.question: What if the longbridge CLI is not installed?
faq.answer: The skill falls back to the Longbridge MCP server if configured. If neither is available, install longbridge-terminal first.

faq.question: Does this skill provide investment advice?
faq.answer: No. The skill returns data and educational strategy frameworks only. All outputs include a disclaimer that the content is for reference and not investment advice.
